title: Layer 1 — Raw Materials & Mining
subtitle: Rare Earths · Metals · Lithium · Copper · Uranium

companies.name: MP Materials
companies.desc: Largest rare earth mining and processing operation in the Western hemisphere. Mountain Pass mine in California produces neodymium-praseodymium oxide essential for the permanent magnets inside AI cooling fans, motors, and electric vehicles that power data center fleets.

companies.name: Lynas Rare Earths
companies.desc: Australia's premier rare earth miner and the sole major non-China processor of separated rare earth materials. Produces neodymium-praseodymium critical for high-performance permanent magnets in AI server cooling and power infrastructure.

companies.name: Freeport-McMoRan
companies.desc: World's largest publicly traded copper producer. Copper is the fundamental conductor for all AI data center power distribution, busbars, cables, and transformer windings. AI buildout is driving a structural uptick in copper demand estimated at 250,000+ tonnes per year by 2030.

companies.name: Rio Tinto
companies.desc: Global mining giant supplying copper, lithium, and boron critical for semiconductor manufacturing and energy storage. Rio Tinto's Rincon lithium project in Argentina targets battery-grade lithium for UPS systems and data center backup storage.

companies.name: BHP Group
companies.desc: Diversified miner with the world's largest copper resource base. BHP's Escondida and Olympic Dam operations supply copper to semiconductor and data center power supply chains.

companies.name: Albemarle
companies.desc: World's largest lithium producer supplying battery-grade lithium used in UPS systems for data centers. Also produces bromine used as a flame retardant in printed circuit boards inside every AI server.

companies.name: Energy Fuels
companies.desc: U.S. uranium and rare earth producer. Expanding rare earth separation capacity at the White Mesa Mill — the only operating conventional uranium mill in the U.S. Uranium is critical for nuclear SMRs powering AI data centers.

companies.name: Piedmont Lithium
companies.desc: Developing domestic U.S. lithium supply from the Carolina lithium project. Beneficiary of CHIPS Act and IRA domestic supply chain mandates.

title: Layer 2 — Semiconductor Materials & Chemicals
subtitle: Silicon Wafers · Photoresists · EUV Blanks · Specialty Gases · CMP Slurries

companies.name: Shin-Etsu Chemical
companies.desc: World's #1 silicon wafer producer with ~30% global market share. Every leading-edge AI chip manufactured at TSMC, Samsung, or Intel Foundry begins with a Shin-Etsu polished silicon wafer. Also dominates photomask blanks for EUV lithography.

companies.name: Sumco Corporation
companies.desc: World's #2 silicon wafer supplier (~25% global share). Critical bottleneck for sub-5nm node AI chip production. Long-term supply agreements with TSMC, Samsung, and Intel provide revenue visibility through 2028.

companies.name: Entegris
companies.desc: Critical materials integrity and contamination control solutions used in every major semiconductor fab globally. Supplies ultra-pure chemicals, filtration systems, and specialty packaging ensuring zero-defect delivery of chemicals used to make AI chips.

companies.name: DuPont de Nemours
companies.desc: Electronic materials division supplies CMP slurries, advanced dielectric films, and chemical mechanical planarization pads critical for AI chip manufacturing.

companies.name: Linde plc
companies.desc: World's largest industrial gas company and the largest supplier of electronic-grade gases to global semiconductor fabs. On-site production facilities adjacent to TSMC, Samsung, and Intel fabs supply ultra-high-purity gases critical for every chip manufacturing step.

companies.name: Air Products
companies.desc: Specialty industrial gases essential in wafer fabrication. Air Products supplies bulk gases used in every CVD, ALD, and etch step of AI chip manufacturing. Its on-site gas generation systems serve TSMC and Samsung fabs directly.

companies.name: Merck KGaA
companies.desc: Electronics division supplies specialty chemicals for semiconductors. One of only three global suppliers of EUV pellicles — the protective membranes covering photomasks used in every sub-7nm AI chip.

title: Layer 3 — Semiconductor Equipment
subtitle: Lithography · Etch · Deposition · Inspection · Metrology · Test Equipment

companies.name: ASML Holding
companies.desc: The sole manufacturer of EUV lithography systems essential for manufacturing chips at 7nm nodes and below. Without ASML, sub-7nm AI chip production globally stops. Each EUV system costs $350M+, contains 100,000 parts, and requires 40 shipping containers to deliver.

companies.name: Applied Materials
companies.desc: World's largest semiconductor equipment company by revenue. Applied Materials' CVD, ALD, PVD, and CMP tools are used in every step of AI chip manufacturing. Directly benefits from every new Blackwell GPU ramp at TSMC.

companies.name: Lam Research
companies.desc: Dominant provider of etch and deposition equipment for 3D NAND, DRAM HBM, and advanced logic. Lam's etch systems define the pattern fidelity of every AI GPU interconnect layer.

companies.name: KLA Corporation
companies.desc: The dominant process control, inspection, and metrology equipment provider. KLA's inspection systems catch defects that would otherwise reduce AI chip yields. Every percentage point of yield improvement at TSMC translates to $100M+ in chip value.

companies.name: Tokyo Electron (TEL)
companies.desc: Japan's largest and the world's third-largest semiconductor equipment company. TEL's coater/developer systems apply and develop photoresists on wafers — a step required for every lithography pass on every AI chip.

companies.name: Teradyne
companies.desc: Leading automated test equipment (ATE) manufacturer critical for validating AI chips post-manufacture. Teradyne's platforms test AI GPUs, HBM memory, and networking ASICs. Also the world's leading collaborative robot maker through Universal Robots.

companies.name: Advantest Corporation
companies.desc: Leading ATE manufacturer and NVIDIA's primary test partner for AI GPU validation. Advantest's V93000 platform is the industry-standard for final testing of NVIDIA H100/H200/Blackwell GPUs and AMD MI300X accelerators.

title: Layer 4 — Chip Fabrication (Foundries) & Advanced Packaging
subtitle: Logic Foundries · Memory Fabs · OSAT · CoWoS · HBM Stacking

companies.name: TSMC
companies.desc: The world's most critical AI infrastructure company. TSMC manufactures virtually every leading-edge AI chip: NVIDIA Blackwell GPUs, AMD MI300X, Apple M-series, and all custom TPU/Trainium/Maia ASICs. Controls ~90% of advanced-node foundry capacity. CoWoS advanced packaging for AI GPUs is fully booked through 2027.

companies.name: Samsung Foundry
companies.desc: World's second-largest foundry and dominant HBM memory producer. Samsung is the primary supplier of HBM3E memory stacked on NVIDIA Blackwell GPUs. Each H200 GPU requires ~141GB of HBM3E from Samsung or SK Hynix.

companies.name: SK Hynix
companies.desc: NVIDIA's primary HBM partner and the world's leading HBM3E supplier. Each NVIDIA H200 GPU contains 141GB of SK Hynix HBM3E. SK Hynix's HBM production capacity is the binding constraint on NVIDIA GPU output.

companies.name: Micron Technology
companies.desc: The only US-based DRAM and NAND manufacturer. Micron's HBM3E is ramping rapidly for NVIDIA Blackwell and AMD MI300X. CHIPS Act recipient building massive fabs in Idaho and New York for domestic AI chip memory production.

companies.name: Intel Foundry (IFS)
companies.desc: Intel's foundry business is the US government's primary bet for domestic leading-edge chip manufacturing. Intel 18A process node with RibbonFET transistors and PowerVia backside power delivery targets 2026 high-volume manufacturing.

companies.name: ASE Technology (ASEH)
companies.desc: World's largest OSAT company. ASE provides advanced flip-chip, wire bond, system-in-package, and fan-out packaging for AI chips. Direct beneficiary of the chiplet architecture shift requiring complex multi-die packaging.

companies.name: Amkor Technology
companies.desc: Major OSAT for advanced packaging for AI silicon. Amkor assembles GPU packages for NVIDIA platforms and holds strategic deals with Apple and TSMC for Arizona-based advanced packaging.

title: Layer 5 — AI Chip Design (Fabless & IDM)
subtitle: GPUs · Training ASICs · Inference ASICs · NPUs · Networking Silicon · CPU

companies.name: NVIDIA Corporation
companies.desc: The uncontested dominant force in AI accelerators. NVIDIA's Hopper (H100/H200) and Blackwell (B200, GB200) GPU architectures power virtually every AI training cluster worldwide. CUDA ecosystem lock-in across 4M+ developers creates a structural moat. Over 80% market share in AI data center GPUs.

companies.name: AMD
companies.desc: NVIDIA's closest challenger in AI accelerators. AMD MI300X and MI325X GPUs are adopted by Microsoft Azure, Meta, and Oracle for AI inference workloads. AMD's open-source ROCm software stack is gaining developer traction as the CUDA alternative.

companies.name: Broadcom Inc.
companies.desc: The dominant custom ASIC designer for hyperscalers — Google TPU, Meta MTIA, Apple neural engine. Broadcom's custom AI chip programs offer 40-65% cost advantage over off-the-shelf GPUs. Also the #1 provider of networking switch chips forming the ethernet fabric of AI clusters.

companies.name: Marvell Technology
companies.desc: Captured custom ASIC design wins from AWS (Trainium) and Microsoft (Maia). Provides high-speed SerDes IP blocks enabling chip-to-chip AI interconnects. Acquired Celestial AI for optical interconnects in 2026.

companies.name: Arm Holdings
companies.desc: The CPU architecture licensing company powering virtually all mobile AI and an increasing share of data center AI compute. AWS Graviton, Apple M-series, Qualcomm Oryon, and NVIDIA Grace all use Arm CPU designs.

companies.name: Apple Inc.
companies.desc: M-series silicon with 16-core Neural Engine leads on-device AI inference. Apple Intelligence features run entirely on-device, setting the benchmark for edge AI deployment at 1.5B+ device scale.

companies.name: Qualcomm Technologies
companies.desc: On-device AI leadership via Snapdragon NPU in smartphones, PCs, and automotive. Snapdragon X Elite enables PC-class on-device AI without cloud connectivity. Expanding into data center AI inference and automotive AI.

companies.name: Cerebras Systems
companies.desc: Maker of the Wafer-Scale Engine (WSE-3) — the largest chip ever built with 4 trillion transistors. Targets AI supercomputing clusters requiring massive single-chip compute. IPO'd in late 2025.

companies.name: Groq
companies.desc: Language Processing Unit (LPU) architecture offering 800+ tokens/second on AI inference. Groq eliminates DRAM in favor of SRAM compute-in-memory for ultra-fast inference speeds.

title: Layer 6 — Networking, Interconnects & Photonics
subtitle: InfiniBand · Ethernet Switches · Optical Transceivers · Cables · Co-Packaged Optics

companies.name: Arista Networks
companies.desc: The dominant provider of cloud networking switches for AI data centers. Arista's EOS and 400G/800G Ethernet switches are the standard for hyperscaler AI networks. Meta, Microsoft, and Google are major customers.

companies.name: Cisco Systems
companies.desc: Building AI-native networking via Silicon One ASIC for AI fabric switching. Acquired Splunk for AI-driven network observability. Repositioning toward AI infrastructure networking for hyperscalers and enterprise AI.

companies.name: Coherent Corp.
companies.desc: Leading supplier of indium phosphide wafers and 400G/800G optical transceiver modules for AI data center interconnects. Every AI data center optical link traces to Coherent's laser and photonics technology.

companies.name: Lumentum Holdings
companies.desc: Leading manufacturer of laser components, VCSEL arrays, and optical transceivers for data center networking. Its coherent optical modules enable the high-speed inter-server links between AI GPU racks and top-of-rack switches.

companies.name: Fabrinet
companies.desc: The de facto contract manufacturer for optical transceivers used in AI data centers. Assembles and tests modules for Lumentum and Coherent. Directly benefits from the surge in 800G/1.6T optical transceiver demand.

companies.name: Credo Technology
companies.desc: Designs active electrical cables and SerDes chiplets enabling 400-800Gb/s interconnects within AI GPU clusters. Revenue surged 272% YoY. Acquired Hyperlume for MicroLED optical interconnects.

title: Layer 7 — AI Servers, Systems & Storage
subtitle: GPU Servers · HPC Systems · ODMs · Storage Arrays · Parallel File Systems

companies.name: Super Micro Computer
companies.desc: The leading AI server ODM specialising in custom high-density GPU servers with integrated direct liquid cooling. Pioneered DLC for NVIDIA Blackwell racks. SMCI assembles and ships more NVIDIA H100/H200/B200 systems than any other vendor.

companies.name: Dell Technologies
companies.desc: Leading AI server manufacturer via PowerEdge XE9680 and the official NVIDIA DGX dealer. Dell's AI Infrastructure Solutions Group is one of the fastest-growing business segments with $15B+ AI server backlog.

companies.name: Hewlett Packard Enterprise
companies.desc: Manufactures AI servers and HPC systems including the Cray EX supercomputer family. HPE's Slingshot high-speed interconnect is used in the world's largest supercomputers. Acquired Juniper Networks for $14B for AI-native networking.

companies.name: Pure Storage
companies.desc: All-flash storage arrays optimized for AI training workloads. FlashBlade delivers the NVMe-over-Fabric throughput needed to feed GPU clusters. NVIDIA DirectGPU architecture partner enabling GPU-direct storage.

companies.name: Western Digital
companies.desc: Leading NAND flash and HDD manufacturer serving AI data centers with high-capacity cold storage. WDC's UltraStar drives and enterprise Ultrastar SSDs are standard in hyperscaler storage arrays for AI data lakes.

companies.name: Seagate Technology
companies.desc: World's largest HDD manufacturer. Seagate's HAMR technology enables 30TB+ capacity drives for AI cold storage. Critical for the massive storage requirements of AI model training datasets.

companies.name: Quanta Computer
companies.desc: Major ODM for Google, Meta, and Microsoft AI server deployments. Quanta's QCT division designs hyperscale-optimized servers and is one of the largest AI server ODMs globally by volume.

title: Layer 8 — Data Centers: Construction, Real Estate & Facilities
subtitle: Hyperscale Builds · Data Center REITs · Colocation · Cooling · Construction

companies.name: Equinix
companies.desc: The world's largest data center REIT and interconnection hub. 260+ IBX data centers in 70+ metros globally. AI demand is driving 30-50% premium pricing on new leases. Power-constrained markets command highest premiums.

companies.name: Digital Realty Trust
companies.desc: Largest pure-play hyperscale data center REIT globally. New lease pricing 30-50% above expiring leases driven by AI demand. Over 300 data centers in 50+ metros worldwide targeting 1GW+ of AI-optimised campuses through 2027.

companies.name: CoreWeave
companies.desc: The leading AI-native cloud (neocloud), IPO'd March 2025. $5.1B revenue in 2025, guiding $12-13B for 2026. Operates the world's largest fleet of NVIDIA H100/H200/Blackwell GPUs exclusively for AI workloads. Microsoft is the largest customer.

companies.name: Vertiv Holdings
companies.desc: The premier integrated provider of AI data center power and thermal management. Makes UPS systems, liquid cooling units (CDUs), and rack infrastructure. Q4 2025 organic orders grew 252% YoY. $15B backlog (book-to-bill 2.9x).

companies.name: Eaton Corporation
companies.desc: Provides electrical systems for AI data centers: switchgear, busways, transformers, and UPS systems. Goldman Sachs top pick for AI power infrastructure. Eaton's UPS products are in virtually every hyperscaler deployment.

companies.name: Trane Technologies
companies.desc: Specialized HVAC and cooling systems for AI data centers. Q4 2025 Americas Commercial HVAC applied bookings up 120%+ YoY. Record enterprise backlog of $7.8B driven by AI data center demand.

companies.name: Iron Mountain
companies.desc: Data center REIT expanding into AI infrastructure. Iron Mountain has 120MW+ of commissioned data center capacity with a 500MW+ pipeline. Project Matterhorn is a $500M+ investment in AI-ready hyperscale development.

title: Layer 9 — Power Generation, Grid & Energy Infrastructure
subtitle: Electricity Generation · Transmission · Transformers · Backup Power · Nuclear · SMRs

companies.name: Constellation Energy
companies.desc: Largest US nuclear fleet operator (21 reactors, 32,000MW+). Microsoft signed a landmark 20-year PPA to restart the Three Mile Island nuclear plant for AI data center power. Nuclear provides 24/7 carbon-free baseload power.

companies.name: Vistra Corp.
companies.desc: Largest competitive US power generator with nuclear and natural gas assets. Acquired Energy Harbor (nuclear) in 2024. Stock up ~400%+ since AI data center power thesis emerged. Direct energy supply agreements with hyperscalers.

companies.name: GE Vernova
companies.desc: GE Vernova supplies gas turbines, grid equipment, and power transformers critical for AI data center power supply. Natural gas turbines are the primary near-term solution to AI data center power demand surge. Goldman Sachs top pick.

companies.name: NextEra Energy
companies.desc: World's largest renewable energy company. Key supplier for AI data center green power purchase agreements. AI hyperscalers are signing 10-20 year renewable PPAs with NextEra at record pace.

companies.name: Quanta Services
companies.desc: The leading specialty contractor for power grid construction. Directly benefits from grid capacity expansion needed to power AI data centers. Builds transmission lines and substations for utilities serving data center campuses.

companies.name: Siemens Energy
companies.desc: Power transformers, switchgear, and grid infrastructure for AI data center connections. Siemens Energy's transformer business faces a 3-4 year backlog as AI data center demand overwhelms production capacity.

companies.name: Cummins Inc.
companies.desc: Diesel and natural gas generator sets for data center backup power. Cummins' QSK95 generators (4.5MW per unit) are the industry standard for hyperscale data center emergency power. Also expanding into hydrogen fuel cell power.

companies.name: Caterpillar Inc.
companies.desc: Generator sets and power systems for data center emergency and continuous backup power. Cat's XQ/SR diesel and gas generator packages are deployed in data centers globally worldwide.

companies.name: Kairos Power
companies.desc: Advanced SMR developer. Google signed a landmark agreement to purchase 500MW of power from Kairos HERMES SMRs — the first-ever commercial agreement between a tech company and an SMR developer.

title: Layer 10 — Cloud Platforms, Foundation AI Models & AI Applications
subtitle: Hyperscalers · Foundation Models · MLOps · Enterprise AI · AI-Native Applications

companies.name: Microsoft / Azure / OpenAI
companies.desc: Microsoft holds exclusive cloud rights to OpenAI GPT-4o, o3, and o4-mini models via a $13B investment. Azure AI platform is the enterprise standard. Copilot AI embedded across Windows, Office 365, Teams, and GitHub.

companies.name: Amazon Web Services
companies.desc: Largest cloud provider ($115B+ annualized revenue). AWS Bedrock hosts 100+ foundation models. Trainium 3 and Inferentia 2 custom chips provide cost-optimised AI training and inference. $700B+ in AI capex commitments for 2026.

companies.name: Alphabet / Google Cloud
companies.desc: Google Cloud runs on TPU-native AI infrastructure. Google DeepMind developed Gemini 2.0 Ultra/Flash, AlphaFold, and Project Astra. $75B 2025 capex commitment. Vertex AI is the enterprise ML platform.

companies.name: Meta Platforms
companies.desc: Largest open-source AI model developer (Llama 4 Scout, Maverick, Behemoth). Meta AI assistant deployed to 3.3B daily active users. $65B 2025 capex. Llama models democratize AI access and commoditize closed models.

companies.name: Oracle Corporation
companies.desc: OCI operates one of the world's largest GPU clusters — contracted to build a 1GW AI data center for Elon Musk's xAI (100,000 NVIDIA GPUs). Oracle Database + AI convergence makes OCI uniquely sticky for enterprise AI.

companies.name: CoreWeave
companies.desc: The defining neocloud of the AI era. $5.1B revenue (2025) guiding $12-13B for 2026. Operates the world's largest third-party NVIDIA GPU cluster. Microsoft is the largest customer ($12B+ contract).

companies.name: Palantir Technologies
companies.desc: AIP and Ontology layer translate AI model outputs into enterprise decisions. The US government's leading AI analytics platform. US Commercial revenue growing 71% YoY. Stock up 370%+ since 2023.

companies.name: Salesforce
companies.desc: Agentforce platform deploys autonomous AI agents handling customer service, sales, and operations. 5,000+ Agentforce enterprise customers. Einstein AI generates $4B+ annual contract value.

companies.name: Anthropic
companies.desc: Claude 3.5/4 model family — leading enterprise and safety-focused AI models. $8B Amazon investment with AWS as primary cloud partner. $4B Google investment. Claude Enterprise is the fastest-growing enterprise AI product.

companies.name: Snowflake
companies.desc: The leading AI data cloud platform enabling enterprises to train and deploy AI models on their own data. Cortex AI runs LLMs directly in Snowflake. Document AI and Cortex Analyst enable natural language enterprise data querying.

companies.name: ServiceNow
companies.desc: Now Intelligence AI integrates throughout enterprise IT, HR, procurement, and finance workflows. GenAI features contributed to 22%+ subscription revenue growth. AI agents automate IT service management without human intervention.
